finding_results.notes: Replaced the two aphoristic Roy lines ('Three too many words' and 'You just haven't figured out how to stop') with office-plain speech. Roy now says 'They called you a comedian' (line 251) and 'You gave one speech, Doug. You were drunk' (line 255). Both lines are factual observations, not diagnoses — they reject the headline's framing by stating what actually happened rather than delivering a thesis about what Doug is. Neither line can be lifted as a standalone aphorism; their force depends on context (the headline, the morning routine, Roy's desk). Restored the grounding 'Morning' exchange from the original snapshot to re-anchor the beat in office normalcy. Removed narrator commentary ('The assessment was complete') that admired Roy's line. Roy's voice matches style bible: short declarative sentences, moderate contractions, economical, no secretly-wise-sage delivery.
